items.original: Меня опять терзают смутные сомнения… У Шпака — магнитофон, у посла — медальон
items.translation: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.reference: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.analysis: Perfect match to the approved/reference translation. It preserves the mock-heroic register of «терзают смутные сомнения» and keeps the clipped parallelism that drives the comedic callback. No issues.

items.original: Здравствуйте, царь, очень приятно. Царь, очень приятно, царь. Очень приятно, здравствуйте, царь.
items.translation: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.reference: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.analysis: Perfect match to the approved/reference translation. The repetitive, banal politeness is preserved and reads as intentionally awkward, which is the joke. No issues.

items.translation: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.reference: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.analysis: Perfect match to the approved/reference translation. Keeps the authoritarian performative force and comic overreach. No issues.

items.original: Оставь меня, старушка, я в печали
items.translation: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.reference: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.analysis: Perfect match to the approved/reference translation. Retains the elevated/archaic melancholy and the incongruous address «старушка» that triggers the humor. No issues.

items.translation: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.reference: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.analysis: Perfect match to the approved/reference translation. Preserves the subject role of “demons” and the panicked, superstitious framing. No issues.

items.translation: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.reference: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.analysis: Perfect match to the approved/reference translation. Keeps the mock-biblical flourish and the religious certainty that creates the comic contrast in a modern setting. No issues.

items.translation: The floor is open to everyone!
items.reference: The floor is open to everyone!
items.analysis: Perfect match to the approved/reference translation. Captures the MC/announcer vibe and the permissive command that fits the scene’s comic chaos. No issues.

items.translation: Now, this was a good call!
items.reference: Now, this was a good call!
items.analysis: Perfect match to the approved/reference translation. Keeps the breezy self-satisfaction and criminal opportunism without over-explaining. No issues.

items.original: Икра чёрная, красная… Да! Заморская икра… баклажанная!
items.translation: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.reference: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.analysis: Perfect match to the approved/reference translation. Crucially avoids calling the eggplant spread “caviar,” preserving the intended joke and preventing confusion. No issues.

items.original: Три магнитофона, три кинокамеры, три портсигара отечественных, куртка… замшевая… Три
items.translation: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.reference: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.analysis: Perfect match to the approved/reference translation. Preserves the escalating inventory-comedy and the slightly incoherent counting that signals exaggeration. No issues.

items.original: И тебя вылечат… и тебя тоже вылечат… и меня вылечат…
items.translation: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.reference: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.analysis: Perfect match to the approved/reference translation. Keeps the calm, sing-song fatalism and shared-absurdity punchline. No issues.
